note: Honest coverage frontier. status: present = a runnable step drives real code and passes (must name a test); pending = specified behavior whose implementation does not exist yet; retrospective_gap = shipped without a driving test (must be zero for governed behavior, INV-22). Phase 1 (T-023-1..7) landed: the seam, the deterministic attributor, the rules-as-data mapping, the PVE reader, the runner wiring, the persistence, and the security routing. The embedded default ruleset is GENERIC (taxonomy->disposition mapping only); site principals + pool carve-outs are supplied by a deploy-time override (TG_ATTRIBUTION_CONFIG) and are never baked into the binary. REQ-2304 dominance is per-record: a positively-unsanctioned actor co-occurring with a sanctioned/self/carve-out record still resolves attributed-suspicious (a carve-out can never mask an intruder into authorized-test). Persistence covers the taxonomy outcome + minimized evidence on session_triage; the matched rule id and candidate set are not separately columned in Phase 1 (a Phase-2 refinement). Phase-2 items (the covered-trail-no-entry disposition, the tracer named step, the journal/k8s/netbox/awx/gitops/docker readers, the expires_at retention job) remain pending.

scenarios.name: Every classification carries a typed attribution derived from reader evidence
An admin-attributed fault stands down to the approver graph and is never auto-undone
A TG-self-remediated incident terminates already-remediated without re-actuation
scenarios.test: TestRunnerAttributionSelfNoopTerminates; TestSelfHealOnPoolHostStaysSelf (core/attribution — self survives a carve-out)

scenarios.name: Absent evidence yields unattributable and the pre-feature ladder unchanged
An unsanctioned-actor change polls with security escalation and is never auto-healed
scenarios.test: TestRunnerAttributionSuspiciousEscalates; TestSuspiciousDominatesContradiction; TestSuspiciousDominatesCarveOut (a carve-out never masks an intruder); TestDispositionForResolution (suspicious keeps security-escalate under a contradiction)

scenarios.name: A covered audit trail missing an entry for an observed mutation escalates as suspicious
An authorized-test outcome traverses every existing gate unchanged
Actor evidence is collected only by registered read-only readers with least-privilege credentials
The PVE task-log reader returns actor evidence for a guest lifecycle change
The PVE reader authenticates with a read-only token distinct from the actuation token
scenarios.test: TestResolveSelfActorUsesActuationCredentialNotReadToken (cmd/worker — self=actuation cred, reader=separate RO token); TestSelfPrincipalFromToken

scenarios.name: A reader error is treated as absent evidence with a recorded warning and never blocks triage
scenarios.test: TestRunnerAttributionReaderErrorDegradesToUnattributable; TestReadFailsClosedOnAmbiguousOrMissingGuest; TestReadDegradesOnShortErrorBodyWithoutPanic (a short auth-failure body degrades, never panics)

scenarios.name: The taxonomy-to-disposition mapping is loadable data with a closed disposition enum
scenarios.test: TestParseConfigDefault (generic embedded default: no site principals/carve-outs); TestParseConfigOverride (deploy-time site override parses); TestClosedEnums (core/attribution)

scenarios.name: An absent or corrupt mapping escalates every non-unattributable taxonomy to a human
A valid carve-out classifies a harness fault authorized-test, heals, and records the attribution
An expired carve-out rule no longer matches and the attribution stands down
Contradictory non-suspicious evidence escalates to the approver graph with all candidates recorded
The attribution outcome and evidence references persist on session_triage and the governance ledger
scenarios.test: migration 0035 + TriageStore.RecordTriage (actor_attribution/actor_evidence columns); recordTriage threading in workflow.go

scenarios.name: The decision tracer surfaces the attribution as a named trace step
Agent free-text can never set or alter the taxonomy value
scenarios.test: TestAttribute (taxonomy derived from typed reader evidence only, core/attribution)

scenarios.name: Persisted evidence is retention-bounded and redacted at every boundary
The journal reader returns typed actor evidence for a privileged host action
The journal reader fails closed when no known-hosts file is configured
An identity fact can never by itself set a taxonomy value
Identity enrichment leaves the deterministic core attributor unchanged
A confirmed live admin is promoted to a stand-down
A disabled admin credential in use is demoted to a security escalation
A directory outage reproduces the Phase-1 classification
Identity data is retention-bounded and redacted at every boundary
scenarios.test: TestWarningsCarryNoPrincipal (ldapident — provider+domain only, no principal); SanctionFacts carries no membership field; identity facts are never persisted (transient in the activity)

scenarios.name: A missing authentication event never moves a classification
The AWX job-history reader returns actor evidence for a playbook-driven change
The NetBox changelog reader returns actor evidence for an inventory change
The GitOps MR-history reader returns actor evidence for a declarative change
